must_have_breakdown.evidence: Candidate managed Salesforce environments (Dev, QA, UAT, Production) at TCS but in a DevOps/Release Engineer capacity, not as a Platform Manager overseeing health, scalability, and platform strategy.

must_have_breakdown.skill: 5+ years experience in Salesforce Platform Management or Salesforce Architecture
must_have_breakdown.evidence: 5 years total experience but focused on DevOps/Release Engineering, not Platform Management or Architecture — the scope and seniority are narrower than required.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Expert-level proficiency with Copado for CI/CD and release orchestration
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Owned end-to-end Salesforce release lifecycle using Copado at TCS for 4+ years; holds Copado Fundamentals I, II, and Copado Certified Consultant certifications.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Deep understanding of Salesforce multi-tenant architecture

must_have_breakdown.evidence: Administered Salesforce org security, profiles, permission sets, and managed Dev, QA, UAT, and Production environments at TCS.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Communication and ability to translate complex technical releases into business-friendly roadmaps
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Coordinated with product owners, QA, and engineering teams for sprint releases, but no evidence of translating technical content into executive or business-friendly roadmaps.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Experience managing Business Critical production environments
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Managed Production environment deployments and implemented rollback strategies, but no explicit evidence of 'Business Critical' classification or premier support tier engagement.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Work with Salesforce's premier support tiers

must_have_breakdown.evidence: Extensive use of Salesforce CLI, SFDX, Metadata API, and Salesforce Inspector across multiple roles.
Enforced quality gates, security scans, and deployment governance, but no evidence of applying Salesforce development best practices or design patterns at the platform architecture level.

reasoning: Borderline — hold pending clarification on platform architecture and development depth. This candidate is a strong Salesforce DevOps and Release Engineer, not a Salesforce Platform Manager. The role demands platform architecture ownership, Salesforce multi-tenant expertise, governor limits management, hands-on development (SOQL, SOSL, Apex, Triggers), and CoE leadership — none of which appear in this resume. What IS strong: Copado depth is genuine and certified (Fundamentals I, II, and Consultant), 4+ years of end-to-end release lifecycle ownership at TCS is directly relevant, and the CI/CD pipeline work is well-evidenced. However, the candidate has zero stated experience with Salesforce architecture, platform design patterns, custom object development, or Salesforce premier support — which are must-haves for this senior role. The current Capgemini role (May 2025) is GitHub Actions-focused and moves further away from the platform management scope required. At best, this candidate fits a Senior Salesforce DevOps Engineer role, not a Platform Manager. Do not advance without confirming hands-on development depth and any architecture or CoE experience.
strengths: COPADO MASTERY: Holds three Copado certifications (Fundamentals I, Fundamentals II, Copado Certified Consultant) and owned end-to-end release lifecycle using Copado across Dev, QA, UAT, and Production at TCS for 4+ years — this is genuine, certified depth, not surface-level familiarity.

CI/CD PIPELINE OWNERSHIP: Designed and implemented scalable GitHub Actions pipelines at Capgemini with a quantified 30-40% reduction in deployment time, plus structured validation and rollback strategies that demonstrably improved production deployment success rates at TCS.

MULTI-TOOL DEVOPS BREADTH: Certified across Copado, Gearset (Gearset Fundamentals), and Flosum (Flosum Certified Professional) — a rare combination of cross-platform Salesforce DevOps tool coverage that few candidates can evidence with certifications.
gaps: NO PLATFORM ARCHITECTURE OR DEVELOPMENT DEPTH: The role requires hands-on Salesforce development (SOQL, SOSL, Apex, Triggers, custom objects) and platform architecture ownership — the resume contains zero evidence of any declarative or programmatic Salesforce development, which is a fundamental disqualifier for a Platform Manager role.

NO CoE LEADERSHIP OR STRATEGIC PLATFORM OWNERSHIP: This role demands establishing a Center of Excellence, federated development strategy, and translating releases into business roadmaps. The candidate operates as an individual DevOps contributor with no evidence of team leadership, CoE build-out, or executive-level communication — a critical seniority gap.
